name: BambooHR
description: HR information system for employee records, onboarding and offboarding. Yields joiner/mover/leaver evidence: hire and termination reports, onboarding/offboarding task checklists and admin access level assignments.

steps.action: Sign in to BambooHR and navigate to Reports > Standard Reports, then run the hires vs terminations, headcount and turnover reports for the collection period
steps.permissions_required: Full Admin, or a custom access level with reporting access
steps.security_note: Use a dedicated least-privilege custom access level for evidence collection rather than a personal Full Admin account — reports contain personal HR data

steps.action: Export each report via the export option (Excel/CSV/PDF) and save with a date-stamped filename covering the joiner/leaver population for the period
steps.audit_note: Ensure consecutive collection periods are contiguous so no hires or terminations fall in a gap

steps.action: For a sample of joiners and leavers, open the employee's record and capture the Onboarding or Offboarding tab showing completed task lists (e.g. account provisioning and access revocation tasks) with completion dates and assignees
steps.permissions_required: Full Admin, or access level with onboarding/offboarding visibility
steps.audit_note: Task completion dates and assignees are the primary evidence that JML procedures were followed

steps.action: Navigate to Settings (gear icon) > Access Levels and capture the list of users assigned to Full Admin and any custom admin access levels
steps.audit_note: This is the periodic access review evidence for the HR system itself — date-stamp the capture

steps.action: Upload the exports and screenshots to the evidence repository with the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: HR exports contain personal data (names, dates, roles) — store only in the approved evidence repository, redact fields not needed for the control (e.g. salary, home address) before uploading

L2.title: BambooHR Shared Custom Reports on a Fixed Cadence

steps.action: Navigate to Reports and create a custom report with fields covering the JML population: employee number, name, job title, department, supervisor, employment status, hire date and termination date
steps.permissions_required: Full Admin, or custom access level with custom reporting rights (custom reports require the relevant BambooHR package)

steps.action: Save the report and share it with the compliance reviewers so every cycle uses an identical, centrally maintained report definition
steps.security_note: Share only with named reviewers — shared reports expose employee personal data to everyone with access
steps.audit_note: A shared saved report gives a consistent, repeatable evidence definition between cycles

steps.action: BambooHR does not offer built-in recurring email delivery for core HR reports — the closest built-in mechanism is the shared saved report exported on a fixed calendar cadence; automate delivery via the API instead (see L3)
steps.audit_note: Record this limitation in the collection procedure so the manual export step on the agreed cadence is understood and evidenced

steps.action: On each scheduled date, open the shared report, export to CSV/Excel and file it in the evidence repository with the run date in the filename
steps.audit_note: Keep a simple collection log (date run, by whom) so missed cycles are visible within one period

steps.action: Quarterly, re-export the Access Levels view (Settings > Access Levels) alongside the report to keep the HR-system access review on the same cadence

Create a dedicated service user in a least-privilege custom access level, sign in as that user, click the account name in the lower-left corner and choose API Keys > Add New Key to generate an API key
steps.permissions_required: The API key inherits the permissions of the user that created it — restrict the service user's access level to the fields needed
steps.security_note: The key acts as that user over live HR personal data: store it in a secrets manager, never in plaintext, and revoke/regenerate it when the owner changes. Repeated use of an invalid key temporarily disables API access (HTTP 403).

steps.action: Authenticate with HTTP Basic auth using the API key as the username and any string as the password, and call POST https://{companyDomain}.bamboohr.com/api/v1/reports/custom?format=csv with a body listing JML fields (hireDate, terminationDate, status, jobTitle, supervisor) and a lastChanged filter
steps.permissions_required: API key from a user whose access level can view the requested fields
steps.audit_note: The lastChanged filter yields a daily delta of joiners, movers and leavers

steps.action: Call GET https://{companyDomain}.bamboohr.com/api/v1/employees/changed?since={ISO8601} (Get Changed Employee IDs) as an independent change feed — each entry marks the employee as Inserted, Updated or Deleted — to cross-check that the report captured all changes
steps.permissions_required: API key from a user with employee data visibility

steps.action: Schedule the collection script via cron or a CI/CD pipeline to run daily, writing date-stamped CSV outputs to the evidence repository
steps.security_note: Transfer over HTTPS only; encrypt evidence at rest — these files contain employee personal data and fall under data protection obligations (UK GDPR), so apply retention limits and access controls to the repository

steps.action: Implement error handling and alerting: treat HTTP 403 as a disabled or revoked key requiring immediate attention, and alert on any failed run for re-collection within SLA
steps.audit_note: A logged failure-and-recovery trail demonstrates the process is controlled

steps.action: Validate each run against expected headcount and prior-day deltas, flagging anomalies (e.g. zero changes for a week, or headcount jumps) for human review
steps.audit_note: Volume validation provides assurance of collection completeness

L4.title: BambooHR Managed Pipeline with Webhooks and Reconciliation

steps.action: Create a permissioned webhook via POST https://{companyDomain}.bamboohr.com/api/v1/webhooks with monitorFields covering the JML fields (employment status, hire date, termination date) and your evidence platform's HTTPS endpoint as the target URL
steps.permissions_required: API key with the webhooks.write OAuth scope, from a service user whose access level can view the monitored fields (webhook data access is limited to that user's permissions)
steps.security_note: BambooHR only delivers webhooks to HTTPS URLs; the receiving endpoint handles employee personal data and must be access-controlled and encrypted at rest

steps.action: Verify the HMAC-SHA256 signature on every webhook delivery using the webhook's private key before accepting the payload
steps.security_note: The privateKey is returned only once, at webhook creation, and cannot be retrieved again — store it in a secrets manager immediately. Reject unsigned or badly signed payloads.
steps.audit_note: Signature verification preserves the evidentiary integrity of received events

steps.action: Run a daily reconciliation sweep — GET https://{companyDomain}.bamboohr.com/api/v1/employees/directory plus the L3 custom report — and diff against received webhook events to detect missed deliveries
steps.audit_note: Webhooks are fire-and-forget; the reconciliation sweep is what makes completeness demonstrable

steps.action: Track completeness metrics — events per day, delivery latency, reconciliation mismatches — and alert when webhook volume drops to zero on a working day or mismatches exceed threshold
Publish a collection health dashboard (events/day, mismatch count, failed reconciliations) and generate an automated quarterly review report covering SLA breaches and webhook key rotation
steps.security_note: Rotate the service user's API key and webhook private key on a defined schedule and after personnel changes
steps.audit_note: The dashboard and quarterly report are continuous evidence of a managed collection process
